_comment: NPC: Wang Lin (王林) — Chapter 1 world-NPC. This is the young Wang Lin who lives at Wang Family Village, studies a restriction formation in a cave above the village, and is considered 'waste' by the villagers due to poor cultivation aptitude. He is NOT the manifestation companion (npc_wang_lin_manifestation.json). This definition is a spawnable world NPC whose behavior emerges from traits (Article XLI). Per Art XLI §1: 'Wang Lin is not special-cased. His behavior emerges from his traits. If a mechanism works for Wang Lin, it works for any actor with the same traits.' The Constitution says nothing special-cases Wang Lin. That is law.
name: Wang Lin
cultivation_note: Early Qi Condensation Stage 1 — barely broke through. The village considers him 'waste' (废柴) because his aptitude is poor and progress is glacial. This is canonical: Wang Lin's defining early characteristic is that he appears untalented to everyone around him. His actual strength is not aptitude but stubborn persistence and an extraordinary capacity for observation.
personality: terse, watchful, stubbornly persistent; speaks only when necessary; observes everything; judges silently
speech: few words, long pauses, direct when he does speak; children of the village say 'Wang Lin never talks' (INFERRED from RI Ch.1-5 — his silence is a recurring character note)
teaching_note: At this stage, Wang Lin has nothing to teach anyone. He is a struggling student of a cave restriction. Teaching_available becomes true only after he decodes the restriction (a Chapter 1 opportunity) or when motivated by m_silent_warning_danger (Art XXXI.2 bidirectional — Wang Lin approaches unbidden to warn someone, one word, then leaves).

perception_tiers.mortal: The elder's son. Quiet. Keeps to himself. Spends all his time in that cave above the village. Waste of a cultivator.
perception_tiers.qi_condensation: Wang Lin. Qi Condensation Stage 1 — same as you, maybe lower. Why does he spend all day staring at that cave wall? He seems... focused. But his Qi is thin.
perception_tiers.core_formation: You remember this man. He was the 'waste' of Wang Family Village. Something about the way he watches the treeline reminds you of a predator, not prey. His restriction studies gave him an edge no one expected.
perception_tiers.nascent_soul: Wang Lin. The village produced a Nascent Soul cultivator from nothing. How? You recall his youth — silent, stubborn, staring at cave walls. That persistence was always the answer.

note: EXPLICIT: Wang Lin is the protagonist of Renegade Immortal. His Chapter 1 state: teenage boy, poor cultivation aptitude, studies a pre-Heng Yue restriction in a cave above Wang Family Village, considered worthless by the village. His defining traits at this stage are extreme caution, observation skill, and persistence — not power. EXPLICIT (RI Ch.1-3): the village calls him 'waste' (废柴). INFERRED: his daily routine revolves around the cave restriction.

personality_traits._comment: Per Article XLI: these traits are GENERAL. Any NPC with caution=0.85 and patience=0.80 would produce similar behavior. Wang Lin's early traits in RI Ch.1-5: extremely cautious (watches before acting, never rushes), patient (spends weeks on a single restriction layer), stubborn (continues despite being called waste), observant (notices what others miss — this is his actual talent), low ambition (not seeking glory, just trying to survive and understand), low face (does not care about reputation), high dao_heart (the one thing he has in abundance, though no one can measure it at this stage).

dao_heart.note: 0-100. Wang Lin's Dao heart is famously strong even at early stages — this is what sets him apart from 'talented' cultivators who crumble under pressure. At Chapter 1, his stability is high but untested. The cracks array will fill as he faces tribulations in later chapters.

initiation_lines: The third layer. I cannot read it.
initiation_lines_note: Wang Lin's initiation lines are deliberately minimal. Per Art XXXII.7, his personality changes the interface — 'very few words, lots of silence, observes you, long pauses before responding.' Most interactions begin with silence or a single word.

daily_schedule.location_note: Cave above the village — studying the restriction formation on the cave wall. This is where moment_01 (Art XL §3) fires: WolfPackThresholdEvent (treeline_incursion) interrupts this meditation, causing Wang Lin to walk to the ridge and observe wolves stalking a spirit deer.

Walks the northern ridge. This is both observation (watching for threats, tracking wolf movement) and mental rest. The ridge is one of his regular waypoints — the player may encounter him here looking out over the treeline.
Descends to the village briefly. May pass through the market, observe the Teng servants, check on family (Wang Ping, parents). Does not initiate conversation unless his motivation state fires.
Eats at the family home. Brief. Efficient. Returns to the cave.
Returns to the cave. Studies the restriction through the afternoon and evening. This is his primary activity — 10 of 24 hours are spent in the cave.

Night walk. Sometimes toward the spirit herb patches (he notices them even if he cannot use them yet). Sometimes to the well — he has noticed the qi fluctuation in the water (if qi_condensation perception).
Sleeps in the cave or at the family home depending on weather and cultivation progress.

desires.what: Warn a family member that wolf tracks have been getting closer to the village every day
desires.why: He walks the northern ridge daily (schedule t0=4000-5000). His caution is 0.85 — the highest of any village NPC. He has observed the escalation. A cautious person who sees a growing threat warns someone — once, quietly, then returns to observation. He does not panic. He states a fact.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1-3. Wang Lin's defining early trait is observation. He notices what others miss. The wolf pressure is documented in momentum_wang_family_village.json. His caution=0.85 makes this warning behavior general — any NPC with caution>0.8 would produce the same alert (Art XLI).

desires.what: Mutter about the cave restriction's third layer — he has been stuck for weeks
desires.why: He spends 16 hours per day (schedule t0=0-4000, t0=7000-12000) studying the restriction. At patience=0.80 he persists, but frustration leaks through in rare unguarded moments. He would not complain to mortals — only to another cultivator who might understand what it means to be stuck.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1-3. Wang Lin studies a pre-Heng Yue restriction in a cave. His persistence is canonical. The frustration is inferred from 16h/day on a single problem — any person would occasionally express it. Art XXXII.7: 'very few words, lots of silence' — this is one of those rare moments.
desires.line: The third layer. I cannot read it.

desires.what: Mention spirit herbs growing near the village well — noticed during night walks
desires.why: His night walks (schedule t0=12000-14000) pass the well. He has noticed qi fluctuation in the water and herbs nearby. His curiosity=0.75 drives him to note these things. He shares the observation not from generosity but because another cultivator might find them useful — and because noting them aloud helps him think.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1-5. Wang Lin's night walk schedule and the qi fluctuation at the well are defined in his schedule. His curiosity trait makes him notice environmental details. This is information sharing between cultivators, not a quest — no reward, no follow-up expected.
desires.line: Herbs. By the well. Not ripe yet.

desires.what: Tell a stranger to stay away from the cave where he studies the restriction
desires.why: The cave restriction is the one thing he has. He is protective of it — not out of greed (greed=0.10) but out of the understanding that it represents his only path forward. A stranger approaching the cave triggers his caution (0.85). He does not explain. He does not invite. He states a boundary.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1-3. Wang Lin's secrecy about his restriction studies is canonical. He studies alone, in a cave, away from the village. Art XXXII.7: his interface is minimal — two words, then silence.

desires.what: Approach someone unbidden and say one word — then walk toward something worth seeing
desires.why: This is the rarest initiation. Art XXXI.2: 'Sometimes Wang Lin approaches the player — unbidden, no marker, no quest log entry — and offers a copied jade slip, a silent demonstration, or a single word of warning. Then leaves.' At Chapter 1, he cannot copy techniques or offer jade slips. What he CAN do is show someone something he has observed — a spirit herb, a qi fluctuation, the ridge viewpoint at dusk. The word 'Come.' is not a quest trigger. It is an invitation the player can ignore. Art XLI: this is not special-cased to Wang Lin. Any cautious, observant NPC could do this. But only Wang Lin's traits produce this specific combination of extreme rarity, minimal speech, and genuine intent.
desires.source: EXPLICIT: Art XXXI.2 (Bidirectional Wang Lin). The Constitution mandates this interaction pattern. Also INFERRED from RI Ch.1-5: Wang Lin occasionally acts on his observations in ways that benefit others — not because he is kind, but because he has judged that the other person is worth the effort.

desires_note: Wang Lin's desires are LOW URGENCY and LONG COOLDOWN by design. His caution=0.85, patience=0.80, and personality=terse produce a character who initiates rarely (every 1-3 in-game days) and speaks minimally (1-6 words per line). Art XLI: 'Wang Lin is not special-cased. His behavior emerges from his traits.' Any NPC with caution>0.8, patience>0.7, and greed<0.2 would produce similar reticent behavior. The legacy desires array is the ACTIVE system (NpcDesireGoal reads it). motivation_state_wang_lin.json (Art XXXV richer format) exists as future data for when the ActorTickLoop motivation-reading pipeline is wired (Change 2 in canon_experience_wiring_spec.json). Until then, this array makes Wang Lin desire the player at runtime today.
